world.title: Inside Ukraine’s secret network of 3D printing farms building bombs
world.description: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

world.title: Von der Leyen waits just 17 minutes to celebrate Orbán’s heavy defeat
world.description: Hungary’s prime minister has spent years railing against the European Commission president.

world.title: Europe’s tallest aspen discovered in south-west Estonia
world.description: South-west Estonia is home to Europe’s tallest aspen, putting a remote corner of Pärnu County on the natural map.

world.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in historic Hungary election
world.description: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Bojar.

world.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work in Strait of Hormuz?
world.description: Donald Trump says that the US is going to start blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

world.title: A president and a pope: Two of the world's most influential Americans at odds over Iran
world.description: Never before has the relationship between Washington and the Vatican revolved around two Americans — specifically, a 79-year-old politician from Queens and a 70-year-old pontiff from Chicago.

world.title: Abuse allegations against lead Democrat shake race for California governor
world.description: Media reports detailing sexual assault allegations against US Representative Eric Swalwell prompt resignation calls.

world.title: Mission Pushed to 2028 Amidst Program Setbacks
world.description: Russia's space program faces significant delays, pushing its next lunar mission to 2028 amidst budget issues, technical failures, and falling behind global competitors like the US and China.

business.description: The Tema Oil Refinery (TOR) is pursuing a multi-track financial restructuring strategy to reduce its staggering US$517 million debt burden, with plans ranging from an independent audit by global accounting firm KPMG to a proposed debt-to-equity conversion requiring cabinet approval. business.title: Trading Plan: Can Nifty 50 defend 24,000, Bank Nifty hold above 55,800 after US-Iran talks fail?
business.description: Sustainability above 24,000 is crucial for the Nifty 50 to move toward the 24,300–24,400 levels in the upcoming sessions. However, failure to hold above this level could lead to some consolidation, with immediate support at 23,800.

business.title: How Snap’s Snapdragon-Powered Consumer AR Glasses Deal At Snap (SNAP) Has Changed Its Investment Story
business.description:
Earlier this month, Snap subsidiary Specs Inc. and Qualcomm Technologies announced a multi-year agreement for Qualcomm’s Snapdragon XR chips to power future Specs augmented-reality eyewear, which Snap plans to bring to consumers later in 2026 with on-device AI, advanced graphics, and multiuser digital experiences.
This deal elevates Snap’s decade-long AR glasses effort from a developer experiment to a consumer hardware push, potentially reshaping how its software, AI, and advertising...

business.title: Why AAR (AIR) Is Up 10.1% After Winning Major USAF Pallet Deals And New DFA Stake
business.description:
In recent days, AAR Corp. secured two U.S. Air Force contracts worth US$450,000,000 to repair and manufacture 463L Legacy Cargo Pallets, while aerospace customers increased orders and restocked inventories amid steadier production.
At the same time, Dimensional Fund Advisors disclosed a 4.9% stake in AAR, highlighting growing institutional interest aligned with the company’s expanding defense and aerospace support role.
We’ll now examine how the new U.S. Air Force pallet contracts could...

business.title: Tesla Compact SUV Plan Revives Affordable EV Focus And Valuation Questions
business.description:
Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) is reportedly developing an all new compact, lower cost electric SUV as part of a refreshed product roadmap.
The vehicle is expected to launch first in China, with plans for wider global availability after the initial rollout.
The model is being designed with driverless first capabilities, while still allowing for a human driven configuration.
This move follows an earlier decision to focus more heavily on robotaxi projects and comes as EV competition in China...

business.title: Sun Pharma’s Reported US$12b Bid Recasts Organon’s Risk Reward Profile
business.description:
Sun Pharmaceutical Industries has reportedly submitted a binding offer to acquire Organon (NYSE:OGN).
The proposed deal would be one of the largest overseas acquisitions by an Indian pharmaceutical company.
The news comes as Organon shares trade around $8.83.
For Organon, with shares at $8.83 and a 22.0% return year to date, this potential transaction lands at a pivotal time. The stock has seen a 38.8% move over the past week and 39.1% over the past month, while the 1 year return reflects a...

business.title: Assessing Paychex (PAYX) Valuation After Revenue Growth And Tapcheck Integration
business.description:
What the Tapcheck integration and recent results mean for Paychex stock
Paychex (PAYX) has been in focus after reporting a 20% rise in total revenue alongside growth in operating income, while also rolling out an embedded earned wage access solution with Tapcheck for its PEO clients.
The Tapcheck agreement integrates real time access to earned wages directly into the Paychex HR PEO platform, using payroll data and machine learning to calculate compliant balances and support on demand pay...

business.title: A Look At Super Group (SGHC) Valuation After Spruce Point Strong Sell Call And Forecast Cuts
business.description:
Super Group (SGHC) (SGHC) shares have come under pressure after Spruce Point Capital Management issued a Strong Sell report, flagging sizable downside risk and citing UK tax headwinds and sports betting volatility.
See our latest analysis for Super Group (SGHC).
The bearish report and trimmed 2026 revenue forecasts come on top of a weak recent patch, with a 1-month share price return of a 4.66% decline and year-to-date share price return of a 12.11% decline. This comes even as the 1-year...

science.title: Artemis II's trip around the moon was a huge success. Now what?
science.description: With NASA's lunar comeback a galactic-sized smash, the space agency already has the next Artemis flight in its sights.

science.title: Unique Honeybees Found Only in Southern California Can Fend Off Deadly Varroa Mites
science.description: A unique hybrid honeybee found only in Southern California has demonstrated the ability to survive and fend off deadly Varroa mites.

science.title: Artemis II: Uniting America Through Space Exploration
science.description: A major leap in space exploration, Artemis II's successful moon mission has captured the imagination of Americans across all divides. From inspiring young astronauts to proving Russian rockets ready, space endeavors unify and give hope. This journey highlights a renewed fascination with outer space and its boundless possibilities.

science.title: Scientists Found Strange "Modern" Structures on the Arctic Seafloor That Should Not Physically Exist
science.description: Researchers descending into giant craters on the seafloor found structures that had no right to be there, modern, active, and completely absent from every map ever made of this region.

science.description: Peak viewing is expected on April 22 and 23, with up to 20 meteors per hour.

science.title: This Small Dinosaur Species Likely Survived Thanks To Its Bursts Of Speed, And It Also Helps To Fill A Massive Evolutionary Gap
science.description: Even the smallest dinosaurs play an important evolutionary role.

science.title: The Craters that Made Us
science.description: What if the same collisions we think of as forces of destruction were actually the spark that created life on Earth? New research published in the Journal of Marine Science and Engineering is making a compelling case that meteor impacts didn't just reshape our planet's surface, instead that they may have built the very cradles where life first emerged.
